article_meta.title: Politics Trump signs dozens of executive or

reasoning: The model’s summary is neutral and factual, avoiding the New York Post’s loaded phrasing and Trump’s rhetorical framing. It lists concrete actions and attributions without adopting emotive language or subjective judgments. Perspectives are represented proportionally to the source, which primarily features Trump’s actions and quotes.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., signed, submitted, withdrew) rather than loaded phrasing from the article.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All claims are directly drawn from reported actions and figures in the article, with no added assertions.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments are presented as fact; evaluative language from the source is omitted.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No heightened or alarming language; tone remains measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assumptions about motives or intentions beyond reported statements.
custom_reasoning.slant: Focuses on enumerating actions; no undue emphasis or favor beyond the source’s content.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes all major actions and the sole perspective present (Trump/administration).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Avoids subjective modifiers used by the source such as ‘avalanche’ or ‘wasted no time.’
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced presentation matching the source’s factual content without extra negative framing.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege or dismiss any source category beyond what the article provides.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source, stripping out rhetorical framing and loaded descriptors.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Accurately reflects the article’s perspectives (primarily Trump’s actions and quotes) without adding or omitting opposing views that were not present.
